name: Astor Piazzolla
description: Argentine tango composer, bandoneon player and arranger
abstract: Astor Pantaleón Piazzolla (Latin American Spanish: [asˈtoɾ pjaˈsola], Italian: [pjatˈtsɔlla]; March 11, 1921 – July 4, 1992) was an Argentine tango composer, bandoneon player, and arranger. His works revolutionized the traditional tango into a new style termed nuevo tango, incorporating elements from jazz and classical music.

concerts.label: Astor Piazzolla and His New Group

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I think it’s going to be more — a typical set might be a tango, an Astor Piazzolla, a bossa nova... I could conceivably play that dream set I was talking about playing, a Piazzolla
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Lorraine Feather
about_quotes.evidence: I might have a template like I’ll say, “Well what about this — I was listening to this Bach fugue when I was writing this” or this Astor Piazzolla piece or this Scott Joplin piece
about_quotes.doc_title: Lorraine Feather interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 22, 2023
